Code Review of My Youth - From the BIOS Beep to Software Engineering

Series overview: a complete record of the 30-day Ironman challenge — from computer basics, network protocols, programming languages, frontend frameworks to career development. Two versions of organization (chronological and topical) help with quick learning and lookup.
【Day 29】Ten Years as an Engineer and the Future
From the "beep" to ten years: ignited by games, broken by frontend, grown by writing, and finally dancing with AI — a record of an engineer's stumbling growth.
【Day 28】Every Student Question Was an Arena for My Growth

Questions aren't obstacles — they're leverage. Every student reply was a chance to fix CSS compatibility, practice JS output, build soft skills, and improve interview readiness. Ideals and reality align in the arena.
【Day 27】Got My Layoff Notice While Enjoying a Long Weekend

After getting laid off, I maxed out my skill tree: built e-commerce in Vue, got grilled on interpreted vs compiled and framework vs library, and finally understood — JS fundamentals are the all-purpose passive skill.
【Day 26】And Just Like That, I Was Forced into Becoming a Frontend Engineer
Knowing how to slice = knowing frontend? Hold on — from forced switch to "actually this is great": HTML the skeleton, CSS the look, JS the nervous system. How static pages come alive.
【Day 25】Why Did I Suddenly Become the HTML and CSS Person?

Survival notes from being unexpectedly assigned to HTML/CSS work. A story that fills in HTML semantics and structure, void vs self-closing elements, and core CSS layout and responsive design — avoiding common compatibility landmines beginners overlook.

The capstone presentation looked smooth, but a second look revealed a hive full of holes — from teacher's heads-up to actual landmines: WannaCry, backup strategies, SQL injection, and column-length DoS lessons learned the hard way.
"PHP? Huh? An elephant?" From C-death to PHP-restored satisfaction: embed HTML, connect to DB, get instant on-screen output. Turns out web apps are this addictive.
【Day 22】The Memory Pointers That Made Me Quit C

JS references are like safety scissors; C pointers are a katana! A few super-short examples to understand addresses, address-of, dereference, and type incompatibility — so one swing doesn't decimate your program.
Beginner-friendly: from eAthena scripts to C's Hello World, data types, and memory size concepts — set the baseline first, then face the real boss: pointers.
